1.1: I wrote the former book, Oh Theophila, about all those things that our Lord Yeshua The Messiah began to do and to teach
1.2: Until the day in which he was taken up, after he had commanded the Apostles whom he had chosen by The Spirit of Holiness,
1.3: Those to whom also he showed himself alive, after he had suffered, by many signs for forty days when he was appearing to them, and he spoke about The Kingdom of God.
1.4: And as he ate bread with them, he commanded them not to depart from Jerusalem, but to wait for The Promise of The Father, “Which”, he said, “you have heard from me.”
1.5: “For Yohannan baptized in water, and you shall be baptized in The Spirit of Holiness, after not many days”.
1.6: But when they were assembled, they asked him and said to him, “Our Lord, are you at this time restoring The Kingdom to Israel?”
1.7: He said to them, “It is not yours to know the times or seasons which The Father has placed in his authority.”
1.8: “But when The Spirit of Holiness will come upon you, you shall receive power. You shall be witnesses to me in Jerusalem and in all Judea, and also among the Samaritans, and unto the borders of The Earth.”
1.9: And when he had said these things, as they saw him, he was taken up and a cloud received him and he was hidden from their eyes.
1.10: And as they were gazing into the sky, as he was going, two men were present standing with them in white clothing.
1.11: And they were saying to them, “Galilean men, why are you standing and gazing into the sky? This Yeshua, who was taken up from you into Heaven shall come in this way, as when you saw him going up into Heaven.”
1.12: And after this, they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount which is called Bayth Zaytay, which is beside Jerusalem and separated from it by about seven furlongs.
1.13: And after they entered, they came up to an upper room in which were staying Petraus, Yohannan, Yaqob, Andraeas, Philippus, Thoma, Mattai, Bar Tolmay, Yaqob Bar Halphai, Shimeon The Zealot and Yehuda Bar Yaqob.
1.14: These all were continuing together in prayer with one soul with the women and with Maryam, Mother of Yeshua, and with his brothers.
1.15: And in those days, Shimeon Kaypha stood among them in the midst of the disciples, and there were assembled there about one hundred and twenty men, and he said:
1.16: “Men, brethren, it was right that the Scripture should be fulfilled which The Spirit of Holiness before spoke by the mouth of David about Yehuda, he who was the guide to those who seized Yeshua,”
1.17: “Because he was numbered with us, and he had a part in this ministry.”
1.18: “This is the one who purchased a field for himself with the reward of sin and he fell on his face on the ground, and all his insides burst from within him and gushed out.”
1.19: “And this is known to all those who live in Jerusalem, and so that field is called in the language of the region, 'Haqel Dama', which is, in its translation, 'Field of Blood'.”
1.20: “For it is written in the book of Psalms, 'His dwelling shall be desolate, and there will be no inhabitant in it, and another will take his ministry.' “
1.21: “Therefore it is right that one of these who have been with us all this time in which our Lord Yeshua went in and out among us”,
1.22: “Beginning with his baptism by Yohannan, until the day that he was taken up from our presence, would be a witness with us of his Resurrection.”
1.23: And they presented two, Yoseph, who was called Bar-Shaba, who is named Justus, and Matthaya.
1.24: And when they prayed, they said, “You, LORD JEHOVAH, know what is in the heart of everyone; show the one which you have chosen of these two,”
1.25: “That he would receive a part of the ministry and Apostleship from which Yehuda withdrew, that he should go to his place.”
1.26: And they cast lots, and it came up to Matthaya, and he was numbered with the eleven Apostles.

2.1: And when the days of Pentecost were fulfilled, as all of them were assembled together,
2.2: Suddenly there was from Heaven a sound like a mighty wind and the whole place in which they were sitting was filled with it.
2.3: And tongues like fire that were divided appeared to them, and they sat on each one of them.
2.4: And all of them were filled with The Spirit of Holiness, and they were going out speaking in various languages, according to whatever The Spirit was giving them to speak.
2.5: But there were men dwelling in Jerusalem who were worshipers of God, Jews from every nation under Heaven.
2.6: And when that noise occurred, the entire populace gathered and was agitated, because each one of them heard that they were speaking in their dialects.
2.7: And they were all marveling and were amazed as they were saying, each to the other, “Are not all these who are speaking Galileans?”
2.8: “How are we hearing, everyone, his own dialect in which we were born?”
2.9: “Parthians and Medes and Elanites, and those who dwell in Bayth- Nahrayn, Judeans and Qapodoqians and those who are from the regions of Pontus and of Asia,”
2.10: “And from the regions of Phrygia and of Pamphylia and of Egypt and of the countries of Libya which are near to Cyrene and those who came from Rome, Jews and proselytes”,
2.11: “And who are from Crete, and Arabians, behold, we are hearing from those who are speaking in our own dialects, the wonders of God.”
2.12: And they were all astonished and bewildered. They were saying one to another, “What is this phenomenon?”
2.13: But others were mocking them as they said, “These have drunk new wine and have become drunk.”
2.14: After this, Shimeon Kaypha arose with the eleven Apostles and he lifted up his voice and he said to them, “Men, Jews, and all who dwell in Jerusalem, let this be known to you and give attention to my words.”
2.15: “These are not drunk as you are thinking, for behold, it is now the third hour.”
2.16: “But this is what was spoken by Joel, The Prophet:”
2.17: “'It shall be done in the last days, says God,' I shall pour out my Spirit on everybody, and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions and your Elders shall dream dreams.”
2.18: “And I shall pour out my Spirit upon my Servants and upon my handmaids and in those days they shall prophesy.”
2.19: “And I shall give signs in the Heavens and miracles in The Earth, blood, fire and plumes of smoke.”
2.20: “The sun shall be turned into darkness and the moon into blood until the great and awesome day of THE LORD JEHOVAH will come.”
2.21: “And it shall be that everyone who calls on the name of THE LORD JEHOVAH shall receive life.' “
2.22: “Men, sons of Israel, hear these words: Yeshua The Nazarene, The Man who appeared to you from God with mighty works and with signs and with miracles, which God did among you by his hand, as you know,”
2.23: “This one, who was separated to him for this, in the foreknowledge and will of God, you have betrayed into the hands of the wicked, and you have crucified and murdered.”
2.24: “But God raised him and he destroyed the destructions of Sheol because it was not possible for him to be held captive in Sheol.”
2.25: For David said about him: “I foresaw my Lord always who was upon my right hand that I should not be disquieted.”
2.26: “Therefore, my heart is delighted and my glory celebrates; also my body shall rest upon hope.”
2.27: “Because you did not leave my Soul in Sheol and you did not give your Pure One to see destruction.”
2.28: “You have shown to me the way of life. You shall fill me, O' Sweetness, with your presence!”
2.29: “Men, brothers, it is permitted me to speak openly to you about the Chief Father, David, who died and also was buried, and his tomb is with us until today.”
2.30: “For he was a Prophet, and he knew that God had sworn to him in an oath: “One from the fruit of your body I shall seat upon your throne.”
2.31: And he saw and spoke before about the resurrection of The Messiah, that he would not be left in Sheol, neither would his body see corruption.”
2.32: “God has raised up this Yeshua, and we are all his witnesses.”
2.33: “He is The One who is exalted at the right hand of God and he has received from the Father The Promise which is concerning The Spirit of Holiness, and he has poured out this gift, which, behold, you are seeing and hearing.”
2.34: “For David had not ascended to Heaven, because he said, “THE LORD JEHOVAH said to my Lord, 'Sit at my right hand',
2.35: “Until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.”
2.36: “Let therefore the whole house of Israel know truly, God has made this Yeshua, LORD JEHOVAH and The Messiah, whom you had crucified.”
2.37: And when they heard these things. They were stricken in their hearts, and they said to Shimeon and to the rest of the Apostles, “What should we do, brothers?”
2.38: And Shimeon said to them, “Return to God and be immersed everyone of you, in the name of THE LORD JEHOVAH Yeshua, for release from sin, so that you may receive the gift of The Spirit of Holiness.”
2.39: “For The Promise is to you and to your children and to all those who are afar off, those whom God shall call.”
2.40: And with many other words he was testifying to them and beseeching them as he said, “Be saved from this perverse generation.”
2.41: And people among them readily received his word, and they believed and were immersed, and there were added that day about three thousand souls.
2.42: And they were continuing in the teaching of the Apostles, and they became partakers in prayer and in breaking of the Eucharist.
2.43: And there was fear in every soul, and many signs and miracles were occurring by the hand of the Apostles in Jerusalem.
2.44: And all those who believed were together and everything they had was communal.
2.45: And those who had a possession were selling it and distributing to each man according to whatever was needed.
2.46: And every day they were continuing in The Temple with one soul, and in the houses they were breaking loaves of bread, and they were receiving food as they celebrated, and in the simplicity of their hearts
2.47: They were praising God as they were given affection before all the people, and our Lord was adding unto the church everyday those who were coming to life.

3.1: And it was that when Shimeon Kaypha and Yohannan went up together to The Temple at the time of prayer, which was the ninth hour,
3.2: Behold, men who were keeping an appointment were carrying one man crippled from his mother's womb, bringing and placing him at the gate of The Temple, which is called Shappira, to be asking charity from those entering The Temple.
3.3: This one, when he saw Shimeon and Yohannan entering The Temple, was begging them to give him charity.
3.4: And Shimeon and Yohannan gazed at him and they said to him, “Look at us.”
3.5: And he gazed at them, as he had hoped to receive something from them.
3.6: Shimeon said to him, “I have no gold or silver, but what I do have, I give to you: In the name of Yeshua The Messiah, the Nazarene, stand up and walk.”
3.7: And he seized his right hand and raised him up, and in that moment, his legs and his feet were restored.
3.8: And he jumped, stood and walked and entered with them into The Temple as he was walking and jumping and praising God.
3.9: And all the people saw as he was walking and praising God.
3.10: And they knew that he was that beggar who sat every day and asked for charity at the gate, which is called Shappira, and they were filled with astonishment and wonder concerning what had happened.
3.11: And as he was holding to Shimeon and Yohannan, all the people, while marveling, ran to them at the porch which is called Solomon's.
3.12: And when Shimeon saw it, he answered and said to them, “Men, children of Israel: why do you wonder at this, or why do you gaze as if it was by our own power or by our authority that we have done this, that this man would walk?
3.13: “The God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, The God of our fathers, has glorified his Son Yeshua, him whom you handed over and rejected in the presence of Pilatus, when he had rightly judged to release him.”
3.14: “But you rejected The Holy One and The Righteous One and you demanded a man who was a murderer to be given to you.”
3.15: “And you killed him, The Ruler of Life, whom God raised from among the dead, and we are all his witnesses.”
3.16: “And by the faith of his Name, this one whom you see and know, he has restored and healed, and faith in him has given him this health before you all.”
3.17: “But now my brothers, I know that because of deception you did this as your Rulers did.”
3.18: “And God has thus fulfilled the thing which before was preached by the mouth of all The Prophets, that his Messiah would suffer.”
3.19: “Repent therefore and be converted, so that your sins will be blotted out, and the times of rest from before the face of THE LORD JEHOVAH will come to you.”
3.20: “And he shall send to you The One who was prepared for you, Yeshua The Messiah.”
3.21: “Whom Heaven must receive until the end times of all those things which God has spoken by the mouth of all his Holy Prophets, who were from ancient times.”
3.22: “Moses said, 'THE LORD JEHOVAH shall raise up to you a Prophet like me from your brothers. Hear him in everything that he will speak with you.'”
3.23: “It shall be that every soul who will not hear that Prophet, that soul shall perish from among his people.”
3.24: “And all The Prophets from Samuel and those who were after him have spoken and preached of these days.”
3.25: “You are the children of The Prophets and of the covenant which God appointed to our fathers, when he said to Abraham, 'By your Seed all the families of The Earth shall be blessed.' “
3.26: “God appointed to you from the first and sent his Son to bless you, if you are converted, and you return from your evils.”

4.1: And when they were speaking these words to the people, The Priests and The Sadducees and the Rulers of The Temple rose up against them,
4.2: As they were angered with them that they taught the people in their preaching about the resurrection from among the dead by The Messiah.
4.3: And they laid hands upon them and kept them to the next day, because evening was approaching.
4.4: And many who heard the word believed, and they were in number about five thousand men.
4.5: And the next day the Rulers and the Elders and The Scribes assembled,
4.6: And also Hannan The High Priest, Kaypha, Yohannan, Alexandrus and those who were from the family of The Chief Priests.
4.7: And when they set them in the midst, they were asking them, “By what power or in what name do you do this?”
4.8: Then Shimeon Kaypha was filled by The Spirit of Holiness, and he said to them, “Rulers of the people and Elders of the house of Israel, listen.”
4.9: “If we today are judged by you for the beautiful thing which happened to an ill man, by what means this man was healed”,
4.10: “Let this be known to you and to all the people of Israel, that in the name of Yeshua The Messiah, the Nazarene, him whom you have crucified, whom God has raised from among the dead, behold, in it and by him this man stands before you whole.”
4.11: “This is the stone which you builders have rejected, and he is The Head of the corner.”
4.12: “And there is no salvation in any other man, for there is no other name under Heaven given to the children of men by which it is necessary to receive life.”
4.13: And when they had heard the discourse of Shimeon and of Yohannan, that they spoke it openly, they perceived that they did not know the scrolls and that they were uneducated, and they were amazed at them and recognized them that they had lived with Yeshua.
4.14: And they saw him who had been crippled, who had been healed, standing with them, and were unable to say anything against them.
4.15: Then they ordered to remove them from their assembly, and they were saying one to another:
4.16: “What shall we do to these men? For behold, a public sign that has occurred by their hands is known to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem and we cannot deny it.”
4.17: “But lest this report proceed more among the people, let us threaten that they do not speak again in this name to anyone of the people.”
4.18: And they called them and ordered them that they should not speak at all neither teach in the name of Yeshua.
4.19: Shimeon Kaypha and Yohannan answered and they said to them, “If it is right before God that one should listen to you rather than to God, you judge.”
4.20: “For we are unable not to speak whatever we see and hear.”
4.21: And they threatened them and released them, for they had not found a cause to pursue their lives, because of the people, because everyone was praising God for what had happened.
4.22: For the man in whom this sign of healing had occurred was more than 40 years old.
4.23: And when they were dismissed, they came to their brethren, and they related to them everything whatever The Priests and the Elders had said.
4.24: And when they had heard, they raised their voice as one to God, and they said, “LORD JEHOVAH, you are God, who made the Heavens and The Earth and the seas and all that is in them.”
4.25: And you are he who spoke by The Spirit of Holiness in the mouth of David, your Servant: “Why have the nations raged and the peoples devised nothingness?”
4.26: “The Kings of The Earth stood up and the Rulers held counsel together against THE LORD JEHOVAH and against his Messiah.”
4.27: For truly, Herodus and Pilatus, with the Gentiles and the mobs of Israel gathered in this city against The Holy One, your Son Yeshua, The One whom you anointed,”
4.28: “To do all whatsoever your hand and your will had ordained beforehand to happen.”
4.29: “Also now LORD JEHOVAH, behold and see their threats, and grant to your Servants that they would be preaching your word openly.”
4.30: “And stretch your hand for healing and for mighty acts and for signs to occur in the name of your Holy Son Yeshua.”
4.31: And when they had prayed and made supplication, the place was shaken in which they were assembled, and they were all filled with The Spirit of Holiness, and they were speaking the word of God openly.
4.32: But the crowds of people who believed had one soul and one mind and none of them was saying that the wealth which he possessed was his own, but they had all things common.
4.33: And those Apostles were testifying with great power to the resurrection of Yeshua The Messiah, and there was great grace with all of them.
4.34: And there was none among them who was needy, for those who possessed fields and houses were selling them and bringing the proceeds of those things that were sold.
4.35: And they were laying them at the feet of the Apostles, and it was given to anyone according to whatever was needed.
4.36: But Yoseph, named BarNaba by the Apostles, which is translated, “Son of comfort”, a Levite from the country of Cyprus,
4.37: Had a field, and he sold it and brought its price and laid it before the Apostles' feet.

5.1: One man whose name was Khanan-Yah, with his wife, whose name was Shappira, had sold his field.
5.2: And he took some of its price and concealed it while his wife was aware of it, and he brought some of the money and placed it before the Apostles' feet.
5.3: And Shimeon said to Khanan-Yah, “Why has Satan filled your heart to cheat The Spirit of Holiness and to hide some money of the proceeds of the field?”
5.4: Was it not yours until it was sold? And after it was sold, again you had power over its proceeds. Why have you set your heart to do this thing? You have not cheated men but God.”
5.5: And when Khanan-Yah heard these words, he dropped dead and great fear came upon all those who heard.
5.6: And young men among them arose and gathered him up, and they took him out and buried him.
5.7: And after three hours had passed, his wife also entered, not knowing what had happened.
5.8: Shimeon said to her, “Tell me if for these proceeds you sold the field.” But she said, “Yes, for these proceeds.”
5.9: Shimeon said to her, “Because you have agreed to tempt The Spirit of THE LORD JEHOVAH, behold the feet of those who buried your husband are at the door and they will take you out.”
5.10: And at that moment she fell before their feet and died, and those young men came in and found her dead. And they took her up and brought her out and buried her beside her husband.
5.11: And there was great fear upon the entire church among all those who heard.
5.12: And there were occurring by the hands of the Apostles signs and many mighty acts among the people and they were all assembled together at the porch of Solomon.
5.13: And none of the other people dared to come near them, but the people were magnifying them.
5.14: And those who were believing in THE LORD JEHOVAH were added all the more, multitudes of men and women,
5.15: So that they were bringing the sick out into the streets lying in litters, that when Shimeon would come, at least his shadow might overshadow them.
5.16: And many were coming to them from the other cities which were around Jerusalem, as they were bringing the sick and those who had foul spirits, and they were all being healed.
5.17: And The High Priest and all who were with him, who were of the doctrine of the Sadducees, were filled with jealousy.
5.18: And they laid hands on the Apostles and seized and bound them in prison.
5.19: Then in the night The Angel of THE LORD JEHOVAH opened the door of the prison and brought them out, and he said to them:
5.20: “Go stand in The Temple and speak to the people all these words of life.”
5.21: And they went out at dawn and entered The Temple and they were teaching; but The High Priest, and those with him, called their associates and the Elders of Israel and they sent to the prison to bring the Apostles.
5.22: And when those who were sent went from them, they did not find them in prison and they returned and came,
5.23: And they were saying, “We found the prison locked securely by the guards who were standing at the door, and we opened it and we found no one there.”
5.24: And when The Chief Priests and the Leaders of The Temple heard these words, they were astonished at them and they were considering what this was.
5.25: And a man came informing them: “The same men whom you shut up in the prison, behold, they are standing in The Temple teaching the people.”
5.26: And the Leaders went with the attendants to bring them, not with force, for they were afraid lest the people would stone them.
5.27: And when they brought them, they stood them before the whole Council and The High Priest began to say to them:
5.28: “Have we not emphatically commanded you that you teach no one in this name? But behold, you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and you wish to bring the blood of this man upon us.”
5.29: Shimeon answered with the Apostles and said to them, “God ought to be obeyed more than men.”
5.30: “The God of our forefathers raised up Yeshua, The One whom you murdered, when you hanged him on a tree.”
5.31: “God has appointed This One The Head and The Life Giver and he has exalted him to his right hand, so as to give repentance and forgiveness of sins to Israel.”
5.32: “And we are witnesses of these accounts, and so is The Spirit of Holiness, whom God gives to those who believe in him.”
5.33: And when they heard these words, they were enraged with a passion, and they were considering killing them.
5.34: And one of The Pharisees there stood up whose name was Gamaliel. He taught The Law, and he was honored by all the people, and he commanded to take the Apostles out for a short time.
5.35: And he said to them, “Men, sons of Israel, take heed to yourselves and see what is right for you to do concerning these men.”
5.36: “Before this time Theuda arose and said that he was something great and about four hundred men went after him; he was killed and those who were going after him were scattered and they became as nothing.”
5.37: “And Yehudah the Galilean arose after him in the days when the people were registered for the head tax and he seduced many people after him and he died and all those who were following him were scattered.”
5.38: “And I say to you, separate yourselves from these men and leave them, for if this counsel and work is from men, they will dissolve and pass away.”
5.39: “But if it is from God, you have no power to destroy it, lest you be found opposing God.”
5.40: And they were persuaded by him and called the Apostles and scourged them and commanded them not to speak in the name of Yeshua, and they dismissed them.
5.41: And they departed from before them, rejoicing that they were worthy to be disgraced for The Name.
5.42: And they did not cease to teach every day in The Temple and in houses, and to evangelize about our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.

6.1: And in those days when the disciples had multiplied, the Hellenist disciples complained against the Hebrews, that their widows were disregarded in the daily ministry.
6.2: And the twelve Apostles called the whole assembly of the disciples and they said to them, “It is not acceptable for us to forsake the word of God and to serve tables.”
6.3: “Search therefore, my brothers, and choose seven men among you upon whom is the testimony and who are full of The Spirit of THE LORD JEHOVAH and wisdom, and we shall appoint them over this matter.”
6.4: “And we shall continue in prayer, and in the Ministry of the word.”
6.5: And this statement was pleasing before all the people, and they chose Estephanos, a man who was full of faith and of The Spirit of Holiness, and Philippus, Procuros, Nicanor, Timon, Parmena and Nicholas, an Antiochene proselyte.
6.6: They presented these before the Apostles, and when they had prayed, they placed hands on them.
6.7: And the word of God was magnified and the number of the disciples was multiplied greatly in Jerusalem, and many people of the Judeans were obeying the faith.
6.8: But Estephanos was full of grace and power and was doing signs and wonders among the people.
6.9: And there arose men from the synagogue which was called the Libertine, and Cyrenians and Alexandrians, and from Qiliqia and from Asia, disputing with Estephanos.
6.10: And they were not able to withstand the wisdom and The Spirit who was speaking in him.
6.11: And they sent men and instructed them to say, “We have heard him speak words of blasphemy against Moses and against God.”
6.12: They stirred up the people and the Elders and the Scribes and they came and they stood around him and they seized and brought him to the center of The Council.
6.13: And they appointed false witnesses who said, “This man does not cease speaking words against The Written Law and against this holy place.”
6.14: “We have heard him say that Yeshua the Nazarene will destroy this place and will change the customs that Moses delivered to you.”
6.15: And all of them who were sitting in the assembly stared at him, and they saw his face as the face of an Angel.

7.1: And The High Priest asked him, “Are these things so?”
7.2: But he said, “Men, brothers and fathers, listen; The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Bayth-Nahrayn, when he had not come to dwell in Haran,
7.3: And he said to him, 'Depart from your land and from the presence of the people of your relatives and come to the land which I shall show you.'
7.4: And then Abraham went out from the land of the Chaldeans and came and dwelt in Haran, and from there, when his father had died, God removed him to this land in which you dwell today.
7.5: And he gave him no inheritance in it, not even a footprint, and he promised to give it to him to inherit for himself and for his seed when he had no son.
7.6: And God was speaking with him when he said to him, 'Your seed will be a stranger in a foreign land, and they will enslave him and will afflict him four hundred years;'
7.7: 'And that people whom they serve in slavery, I will judge.' God said, 'After these things, they will go out and they will serve me in this place.'
7.8: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision, and then he begot Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day and Isaac begot Jacob and Jacob begot our twelve fathers.
7.9: And our fathers were jealous of Joseph and they sold him into Egypt and God was with him.
7.10: And he saved him from all his sufferings and he gave him grace and wisdom before Pharaoh The King of Egypt and he appointed him Ruler over Egypt and over his whole house.
7.11: And there was a famine and great distress in all of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, and there was nothing to satisfy our fathers.
7.12: When Jacob heard that there was produce in Egypt, he sent our fathers first.
7.13: And when they went the second time, Joseph made himself known to his brothers and the family of Joseph was made known to Pharaoh.
7.14: Joseph sent and brought his father Jacob and all his family, and they were seventy five souls in number,
7.15: ( And Jacob went down to Egypt and died there, he and our fathers, )
7.16: And he was moved to Shechem and was placed in a tomb, which Abraham had bought with silver from the sons of Hamor.
7.17: And when the time had arrived which God had promised with an oath to Abraham, the people had multiplied and had grown strong in Egypt,
7.18: Until another King arose over Egypt who did not know Joseph.
7.19: He was deceptive toward our race and did evil to our fathers and commanded to cast out their male infants that they would not live.
7.20: At that time Moses was born, and was dear to God and was raised three months in his father's house.
7.21: When he was cast out by his mother, the daughter of Pharaoh found him and she raised him as her son.
7.22: And Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was prepared in his words and in his deeds also.
7.23: When he was forty years old, it came upon his heart to visit his brethren and the children of Israel.
7.24: And he saw one of the sons of his tribe being compelled by violence and he avenged him and executed justice for him and killed the Egyptian who had wronged him.
7.25: And he hoped that his brethren, the sons of Israel, would have understood that God would give them deliverance by his hand, and they did not understand.
7.26: And the next day he appeared to them when they contended with one another and he appealed to them to be reconciled when he said: “Men, brothers, why are you doing wrong to one another?”
7.27: But he who was doing wrong to his fellow thrust him away from him and said to him, “Who appointed you the Ruler and Judge over us?”
7.28: “Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?”
7.29: And Moses fled at this saying and he became a nomad in the land of Midian and he had two sons.
7.30: And when forty years were fulfilled to him, The Angel of THE LORD JEHOVAH appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai in the fire which burned in a bush.
7.31: And when Moses saw it, he marveled at the vision, and when he approached to see, THE LORD JEHOVAH said to him in a voice:
7.32: 'I AM THE LIVING GOD, The God of your fathers, The God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob', and as Moses was trembling, he did not dare to gaze at the vision.
7.33: And THE LORD JEHOVAH said to him, 'Loose your shoes from your feet, for the ground on which you stand is holy.'
7.34: 'I have looked and have seen the suffering of my people who are in Egypt and I have heard its groans and I have come down to save them; and now come, I shall send you to Egypt.'
7.35: This Moses, whom they rejected when they were saying, 'Who appointed you the Ruler and Judge over us?', this one God sent as the Ruler and Deliverer to them by the hand of The Angel who appeared to him at the bush.
7.36: This is he who sent them out when he did signs and wonders and mighty works in the land of Egypt and at The Sea of Reeds and in the wilderness forty years.
7.37: This is Moses who said to the children of Israel, 'THE LORD JEHOVAH God shall appoint you a Prophet like me from your brethren. You shall listen to him.'
7.38: This is he who was in the assembly in the wilderness with The Angel who had spoken with him and with our fathers at the mountain of Sinai, and he was receiving the living words which he would give to us.
7.39: And our fathers chose not to obey him, but they forsook him and in their hearts they returned to Egypt,
7.40: As they said to Aaron, 'Make gods for us to go before us, because we do not know what has become of this Moses who brought us out from the land of Egypt.'
7.41: And they made a calf for themselves in those days, and they sacrificed sacrifices to the idol and they were delighting in the work of their hands.
7.42: And God turned and handed them over to become worshipers of the hosts of the Heavens, just as it is written in the Scriptures of The Prophets, 'Why have you brought animals or sacrifices to me for forty years in the wilderness, sons of Israel?'
7.43: 'But you carry the tabernacle of Malcom and the star of The God Rephan, images which you have made to worship. I shall remove you farther than Babel.'
7.44: Behold, the tabernacle of the testimony of our fathers was in the wilderness according to what he who spoke with Moses commanded to make it, with the likeness which he showed him.
7.45: And our fathers brought the same tabernacle and carried it with Joshua to the land that God had given them as an inheritance from the nations which he had driven out from before them, and it was carried until the days of David,
7.46: He who found affection before him who is God, and he requested to find a tabernacle for The God of Jacob.
7.47: But Solomon built a house for him.
7.48: And The Exalted One does not dwell in the product of hands as The Prophet says:
7.49: 'Heaven is my throne and The Earth is the stool which is under my feet. Where is the house that you will build for me?, says THE LORD JEHOVAH, or where is the place of my rest?'
7.50: 'Behold, has not my hand made all these things?'
7.51: Oh, stiff necks, who are uncircumcised in their hearts and in their hearing, you always are opposing The Spirit of Holiness, as your fathers did, so do you!
7.52: Which of The Prophets have not your fathers persecuted and murdered, who before had searched out concerning the coming of The Righteous One, him whom you have betrayed and killed?
7.53: You have received The Law by the visitation of Angels and have not kept it.”
7.54: And when they heard these things, they were filled with rage in their souls and they were gnashing on him with their teeth.
7.55: And he, being filled with faith and The Spirit of Holiness, gazed into Heaven and he saw the glory of God, and Yeshua as he stood at the right side of God.
7.56: And he said, “Behold, I see the Heavens opening and The Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.”
7.57: And they yelled with a loud voice and stopped up their ears, and they all rushed upon him.
7.58: And they seized and brought him outside the city, and they were stoning him, and those who testified against him placed their robes at the feet of a young man, one who is called Shaul.
7.59: And they were stoning Estephanos as he prayed and said, “Our Lord Yeshua, accept my spirit!”
7.60: And when he knelt down, he cried out in a loud voice and he said, “Our Lord, do not let this sin stand against them!” When he had said this, he fell asleep.

8.1: But Shaul was consenting and participating in his murder, and in that day there was great persecution toward the church which was in Jerusalem, and they were all scattered into the villages of Judea, and among the Samaritans, except for the Apostles.
8.2: And believing men gathered up and buried Estephanos, and they grieved over him greatly.
8.3: But Shaul was persecuting the Church of God, as he was entering houses and dragging men and women and delivering them to prison.
8.4: Those who were scattered were traveling and preaching the word of God.
8.5: But Philippus went down to the city of the Samaritans and he was preaching to them about The Messiah.
8.6: And as they were listening to his message, the people who were listening were persuaded by all that he had said, for they saw the signs that he did.
8.7: For many who had been seized by foul spirits were screaming with a loud voice, and they were coming out from them, and others who were paralytic and crippled were healed.
8.8: And there was great joy in that city.
8.9: But there was a man there whose name was Simon who had dwelt there in that city for a long time. By his sorcery he deceived the people of the Samaritans as he was magnifying himself and saying, “I am the great God.”
8.10: And they were all praying to him, noble and common, and they were saying, “This is the great power of God.”
8.11: And they were all persuaded by him, because many times he had astonished them by his sorceries.
8.12: When they believed Philippus, who was proclaiming The Kingdom of God in the name of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, they were being baptized, men and women.
8.13: Simon also believed and was baptized and was joined to Philippus, and when he saw the signs and the great miracles that were done by his hand, he marveled and was astonished.
8.14: And when the Apostles of Jerusalem heard that the people of the Samaritans had received the word of God, they sent Shimeon Kaypha and Yohannan to them.
8.15: And they went down and prayed over them so that they would receive The Spirit of Holiness.
8.16: For he was upon none of them yet, but they had only been baptized in the name of our Lord Yeshua.
8.17: And they were laying hands upon them, and they were receiving The Spirit of Holiness.
8.18: And when Simon saw that by laying on of the hands of the Apostles The Spirit of Holiness was given, he brought silver to them,
8.19: As he said, “Give me also this authority that he on whomever I place a hand will receive The Spirit of Holiness.”
8.20: Shimeon Kaypha said to him, “Your silver will go with you to destruction, because you thought that the gift of God is acquired by the possessions of the world.”
8.21: “You have no part or allotment in this faith, because your heart is not upright before God.
8.22: But turn from this your evil and request from God that perhaps the treachery of your heart may be forgiven you.
8.23: For I see that you are in the wrath of bitterness and in a knot of evil.”
8.24: Simon answered, and he said, “Ask God for my sake, that none of these things which you have said may come upon me.”
8.25: But Shimeon and Yohannan, when they had testified and taught the word of God, returned to Jerusalem. And they preached The Gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
8.26: And The Angel of THE LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Philippus and he said to him, “Arise, go to the south by the desert road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
8.27: And he arose to go and met a certain Eunuch who had come from Kush, an official of Qandiqe, Queen of the Kushites, and he was authorized over all her treasury and had come to worship in Jerusalem.
8.28: As he turned to go, he sat down on the chariot and was reading in Isaiah The Prophet.
8.29: And The Spirit said to Philippus, “Approach and join the chariot.”
8.30: And as he approached, he heard what he read in Isaiah The Prophet and he said to him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
8.31: And he said, “How can I understand unless a man will instruct me?” And he asked Philippus to come up and sit with him.
8.32: But the section of Scripture he read had this in it: “He was led as a lamb for sacrifice and as a ewe before its shearer is silent, also in this way he opened not his mouth.”
8.33: “He was led in his humility from imprisonment and from judgment and who will narrate his time? For his life is taken from The Earth.”
8.34: And that Eunuch said to Philippus, “I beg of you, about whom did The Prophet say this, about himself or about another man?”
8.35: Then Philippus opened his mouth and began from this Scripture to preach about our Lord Yeshua to him.
8.36: And as they were going on the road, they were arriving at one place that had water in it, and that Eunuch said, “Behold, water! What is the hindrance to me being baptized?”
8.37: And Philippus said, “If you believe with all the heart, it is authorized.” And he answered and said, “I do believe that Yeshua The Messiah is The Son of God.”
8.38: And he commanded to stop the chariot and the two of them went down to the water and Philippus baptized that Eunuch
8.39: And when they had come up from the water, The Spirit of THE LORD JEHOVAH took Philippus up and the Eunuch did not see him again, but he went on the road rejoicing.
8.40: But Philippus was found in Azotus and from there he was traveling and preaching in all the cities until he came to Qesarea.

9.1: But Shaul was full of menace and the fury of murder against the disciples of Our Lord.
9.2: And he requested a letter for himself from The High Priest and to give it for Darmsuq for the synagogues, that if he were to find those following in this way, men or women, he may bind and bring them to Jerusalem.
9.3: And as he was going, he began to approach Darmsuq; suddenly there was light from the Heavens shining upon him,
9.4: And he fell upon the ground and he heard a voice which said to him: “Shaul, Shaul, why do you persecute me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads.”
9.5: He answered and said, “Who are you my lord?” And our Lord said, “I AM THE LIVING GOD, Yeshua the Nazarene, He whom you are persecuting.”
9.6: “But arise and enter the city, and there it will be told you what you must do.”
9.7: And the men who were traveling with him on the road were standing amazed because they were hearing the sound only, but The Man was not visible to them.
9.8: And Shaul got up off the ground and could not see anything, while his eyes were open. And while they held his hands they brought him to Darmsuq.
9.9: And he could see nothing for three days, and he did not eat or drink.
9.10: But there was a disciple in Darmsuq and his name was Khanan-Yah and THE LORD JEHOVAH had said to him in a vision, “Khanan-Yah”, and he said, “Behold, it is I, my Lord.”
9.11: And our Lord said to him, “Arise, go to the street which is called Straight and inquire at the house of Yehuda for Shaul, who is from the city Tarsus, for behold, he is praying.”
9.12: “He sees in a vision a man whose name is Khanan-Yah entering and laying a hand upon him so that his eyes will be opened.”
9.13: And Khanan-Yah said, “My Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he has inflicted on the Saints in Jerusalem.”
9.14: “And behold, he also has authority here from The Chief Priests to imprison all those who call upon your name.”
9.15: And THE LORD JEHOVAH said to him, “Arise, go, because he is a chosen vessel to me to take my name to the Gentiles and Kings and among the children of Israel.”
9.16: “For I shall show him how much he is going to suffer for my name.”
9.17: Then Khanan-Yah went to the house to him and laid a hand upon him and said to him, “Shaul, my brother, our Lord Yeshua who appeared to you on the road, when you came, has sent me so that your eyes would be opened and you would be filled with The Spirit of Holiness.”
9.18: And immediately, some things that were like scales fell from his eyes and his eyes were opened, and he arose and he was baptized.
9.19: And he received food and was strengthened and he was with those disciples who were in Darmsuq for some days.
9.20: And within an hour he was preaching in the synagogue of the Jews about Yeshua, that he is The Son of God.
9.21: And all those who heard him were astounded and they were saying, “Was this not he who was persecuting all those who are calling on this Name in Jerusalem? And he was even sent here to bind them to convey them to The Chief Priests!”
9.22: But Shaul was strengthened even more and was agitating those Jews who were dwelling in Darmsuq as he was showing that This One is The Messiah.
9.23: And as the days progressed, the Jews produced a plot against him to kill him.
9.24: But the plot was shown to Shaul, what they were seeking to do to him. And they were watching the gates of the city day and night that they might kill him.
9.25: Then the disciples put him in a basket and let him down from the wall by night.
9.26: And he went on to Jerusalem, and he wanted to join the disciples and they were all afraid of him and they did not believe that he was a disciple.
9.27: But BarNaba took him and brought him to the Apostles and he related to them just how he had seen THE LORD JEHOVAH on the road and that he had spoken with him on the road, and how in Darmsuq he had spoken openly in the name of Yeshua.
9.28: And he was entering and exiting with them in Jerusalem.
9.29: And he was speaking in the name of Yeshua openly and disputing with the Jews who knew Greek, but they were seeking to kill him.
9.30: And when the brethren knew, they brought him by night to Qesaria, and from there they sent him to Tarsus.
9.31: However, the church that was in all Judea and in Galilee and in Samaria had peace, and it was built up and was proceeding in the worship of God and was growing in the comfort of The Spirit of Holiness.
9.32: And it happened that when Shimeon was traveling among the cities, he came down also to The Holy Ones who dwelt in the city Lud.
9.33: And he found one man whose name was Annis who was lying in bed and had been paralyzed for eight years.
9.34: And Shimeon said to him, “Annis, Yeshua The Messiah is healing you. Stand and make your bed”, and at that moment he got up.”
9.35: And all of those who dwelt in Lud and in Sarona saw and they were converted to God.
9.36: There was a female disciple whose name was Tabitha, in the city, Joppa. She was rich in good works and in the charity which she was doing.
9.37: But she was sick in those days, and she died. They washed her and placed her in an upper room.
9.38: And the disciples heard that Shimeon was in Lud, that city which is beside Joppa, and they sent two men to request of him, “Do not neglect to come with them.”
9.39: And Shimeon arose and went with them, and when he came they took him up to the upper room and all the widows were gathered standing around him, weeping and showing him the coats and cloaks which Tabitha had given them when she was alive.
9.40: But Shimeon sent all of the people outside and bowed on his knees and prayed, and he turned to the corpse and said, “Tabitha, get up”, but she opened her eyes, and when she saw Shimeon, she sat up.
9.41: And he reached his hand toward her and raised her up, and he called the Saints and the widows and he gave her to them alive.
9.42: And this was made known in the whole city, and many believed in Our Lord.
9.43: But he was in Joppa not a few days while he dwelt in the house of Shimeon a Tanner.

10.1: But in Qesaria was a certain man, a Centurion whose name was Cornelius, from the regiment which is called Italiqa.
10.2: And he was righteous and was a worshiper of God, he and his whole house, and he did great charitable work among the people and always prayed to God.
10.3: This man saw an Angel of God in a vision plainly before his face at the ninth hour of the day, who entered his presence and said to him, “Cornelia.”
10.4: And he gazed at him and was afraid and said, “What, my lord?” And The Angel said to him, “Your prayer and your charity have gone up for a remembrance before God.
10.5: “And now send a man to the city Joppa and bring Shimeon who is called Kaypha.
10.6: Behold, he dwells in the house of Shimeon a Tanner which is on the seaside.”
10.7: When The Angel went who had spoken with him, he called two of the men of his household and one Servant who worshiped God, who was agreeable to him,
10.8: And he related to them everything that he had seen and he sent them to Joppa.
10.9: And the next day, when they were traveling on the road and came near to the city, Shimeon went up to the roof to pray at the sixth hour.
10.10: And he was hungry and he wanted to eat, and as things were being gotten for him, he fell into a trance,
10.11: And he saw Heaven as it was opened, and a garment which was tied at the four corners like a great linen, and it was descending from Heaven to The Earth,
10.12: And there were in it all kinds of four footed animals and creeping things of The Earth and birds of the sky;
10.13: And a voice came to him saying, “Shimeon, arise, slay and eat.”
10.14: And Shimeon said, “Never, my Lord, because I have never eaten anything defiled or polluted.”
10.15: And a voice came again a second time to him: “Those things which God has purified you shall not make impure.”
10.16: This happened three times and the garment was taken up to Heaven.
10.17: And as Shimeon wondered in himself what this vision was that he had seen, those men arrived who were sent from Cornelius, and they asked for the house in which Shimeon dwelt and they came and stood at the gate of the courtyard.
10.18: And they were calling there asking, “Is Shimeon who is called Kaypha lodging here?”
10.19: And as Shimeon was pondering the vision, The Spirit said to him, “Behold, three men are seeking you.
10.20: Arise, go down and go with them, doubting nothing in your mind, because I am he who has sent them.”
10.21: Then Shimeon went down to those men and said to them, “I am he whom you seek. What is the reason for which you have come?”
10.22: They said to him, “A certain man whose name is Cornelius, a good Centurion and worshiper of God, for whom all the people of the Jews vouch, was told in a vision by a holy Angel to send and bring you to his house and hear a message from you.”
10.23: And Shimeon brought them in and received them where he was lodging, and the day after he arose and went out with them, and some men of the brethren of Joppa went with him.
10.24: And the next day he entered Qesaria, but Cornelius had been waiting for them while all of his relatives and close friends whom he had were gathered to him.
10.25: And when Shimeon entered, Cornelius met him and fell worshiping at his feet.
10.26: And Shimeon raised him up and said to him, “Stand up; even I am human.”
10.27: And as he spoke with him, he entered and found many who had come there.
10.28: And he said to them, “You know that a man who is of the Jews is not allowed to join a foreign person, who is not one of his race. God has shown me that I should not say that a man is unclean or defiled.”
10.29: “Because of this, I have readily come when you sent after me. However, I ask you why you have sent after me.”
10.30: Cornelius said to him, “It has been four days now since I was fasting, and at the ninth hour, while I was praying in my house, a certain man stood in front of me, being clothed in white.”
10.31: He said to me, 'Cornelius, your prayer is heard and your charity is a memorial before God.
10.32: However, send to the city Joppa and bring Shimeon who is called Kaypha. Behold, he dwells in the house of Shimeon, a Tanner, upon the seaside, and he will come speak with you.'
10.33: And immediately I sent to you and you have done well to come, and behold, we are all before you and wish to hear everything whatever is commanded you from the presence of God.”
10.34: But Shimeon opened his mouth and he said, “In truth, I understand that God is no respecter of persons.
10.35: But among all nations, whoever worships him and works righteousness is acceptable to him.
10.36: For the word which he sent to the children of Israel and announced good news of peace and tranquility to them by Yeshua The Messiah, ( This One is LORD JEHOVAH of all ),
10.37: You also are aware of the word that has come to all Judea, which went out from Galilee after the baptism that Yohannan preached,
10.38: About Yeshua who was from Nazareth, whom God anointed with The Spirit of Holiness and with power, and he was traveling and healing those injured by The Evil One, because God was with him.
10.39: And we witnessed him concerning all whatsoever he did in all the land of Judea and of Jerusalem. The Judeans hanged him on a tree and murdered him.
10.40: And God raised him up the third day and granted him to appear publicly
10.41: But not to all the people, but to us who were chosen by God to be witnesses for him, we who ate and drank with him after his resurrection from the grave.
10.42: And he commanded us to preach and to testify to the people that This is He who was appointed by God to be The Judge of the living and of the dead.
10.43: And all The Prophets have borne him witness, that everyone who believes in his Name will receive forgiveness of sins.”
10.44: And when Shimeon was speaking these words, The Spirit of Holiness rested on all of those who were hearing the word.
10.45: And circumcised brethren who had come with him were stupefied and astonished that the gift of The Spirit of Holiness was poured forth also upon Gentiles,
10.46: For they heard them speaking in various languages and magnifying God, and Shimeon was saying:
10.47: “Can anyone refuse water that they should not be baptized? For, behold, they have received The Spirit of Holiness, as when we did.”
10.48: Then he commanded them to be baptized in the name of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, and they begged him to stay with them for some days.

11.1: And it was heard by the Apostles and by the brethren who were in Judea that Gentiles had also received the word of God.
11.2: And when Shimeon came up to Jerusalem, those who were of the circumcision were contending with him,
11.3: As they were saying that he had entered into the presence of the uncircumcised and had eaten with them.
11.4: And Shimeon interjected, in order to say to them:
11.5: “When I was praying at Joppa, I saw in a vision a garment descending which was like a linen and was tied at the four corners, and it was coming down from the sky and it came unto me.
11.6: I stared at it and I saw beasts in it, four footed animals and creeping things of The Earth and birds of the sky.
11.7: And I heard a voice that said to me, 'Shimeon, arise, slay and eat.'
11.8: And I said, 'Never, my Lord, because what is polluted or defiled has never entered my mouth.'
11.9: And again a voice said to me from Heaven: 'Whatever God has purified, you shall not make defiled.'
11.10: This happened three times, and everything was taken up to Heaven.
11.11: At that moment, three men who were sent to me from Cornelius of Qesaria came and stood at the gate of the courtyard where I was dwelling.
11.12: And The Spirit said to me, 'Go with them without doubting.' And these six brothers also came with me and we entered the man's house.
11.13: He related to us how he had seen an Angel in his house, who stood and said to him, 'Send to Joppa the city and bring Shimeon who is called Kaypha,
11.14: And he will speak with you words by which you will receive life, you and all your household.'
11.15: And as I was continuing to speak there, The Spirit of Holiness rested upon them as when upon us from the first.”
11.16: And I was reminded of the word of Our Lord, which he had said: 'Yohannan baptized in water, but you shall be baptized in The Spirit of Holiness.'
11.17: If God therefore has equally given The Gift to those Gentiles who have believed in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, as also to us, who am I that I would be qualified to forbid God?”
11.18: And when they heard these words, they were silent; and then they praised God and they were saying, “Perhaps God has given a restoration to life to the Gentiles also.”
11.19: But those who were scattered by the suffering which occurred concerning Estephanos had reached Phoenicia and the region of Cyprus and unto Antiakia, when they were speaking the word with no one but the Jews.
11.20: Some of them there were from Cyprus and from Qorina who went in to the Antiochenes, and they were speaking with the Hellenists and preaching concerning our Lord Yeshua.
11.21: And the hand of THE LORD JEHOVAH was with them, and many believed and were turned to THE LORD JEHOVAH.
11.22: And this was heard by the ears of the members of the Church of Jerusalem and they sent BarNaba to the Antiochenes.
11.23: And when he had come there and had seen the grace of God, he rejoiced and he was exhorting them that with all their heart they would cleave to Our Lord.
11.24: For he was a good man, and he was filled with The Spirit of Holiness and with faith, and many people were added to Our Lord.
11.25: And he was going to Tarsus to seek Shaul;
11.26: And when he found him, he brought him with him to Antiakia, and they were assembling together for a full year with the church and they taught many people. From that time, the disciples were first called Christians by the Antiochenes.
11.27: And in those days Prophets came there from Jerusalem.
11.28: And one of them stood up whose name was Agabus and instructed them by The Spirit that a great famine would occur in the whole land and that this famine would be in the days of Claudius Caesar.
11.29: But the disciples set aside, according to what each one of them had, to send for the service of the brethren who dwelt in Judea;
11.30: They sent by the hand of BarNaba and Shaul to the Elders who were there.

12.1: But at that time, King Herodus, who was surnamed Agrippa, was laying hands on the people who were in the churches, to do evil to them.
12.2: And he murdered Yaqob the brother of Yohannan with the sword.
12.3: And when he saw this pleased the Judeans, he proceeded to seize also Shimeon Kaypha, and they were the days of Unleavened Bread.
12.4: And he arrested him and cast him into prison and delivered him unto sixteen Soldiers to guard him so that after Passover he would deliver him to the people of Judea,
12.5: And while Shimeon was guarded in prison, continual prayer was offered by the church for him to God,
12.6: And that night toward dawn, he was preparing to hand him over while Shimeon was asleep between two Soldiers and was bound with two chains, and the others were keeping the gate of the prison.
12.7: The Angel of THE LORD JEHOVAH stood over him, and the light shone in the entire place and he jabbed him in the side and raised him up and said to him, “Arise quickly.” And the chains fell from his hands.
12.8: And the Angel said to him, “Wrap your garment around your waist and put on your sandals”, and he did so; again, he said to him, “Wrap your cloak and follow me.”
12.9: And he went out and followed him while he did not know that this was real which was happening by the hand of The Angel, for he thought that he was seeing a vision.
12.10: And as they passed the first guard and the second they came to an iron gate, and it opened to them of its own accord, and when they went forth past one street, The Angel departed from him.
12.11: And Shimeon realized and said, “Now I know in truth that THE LORD JEHOVAH has sent his Angel to save me from the hand of Herodus The King and from that which the Judeans were plotting against me.”
12.12: And as he pondered this, he came to the house of Maryam, the mother of Yohannan who was surnamed Marqus, because many brethren were gathered there and praying.
12.13: And he knocked at the door of the courtyard and a girl went out to answer it whose name was Rhoda.
12.14: She recognized the voice of Shimeon and in her joy she did not open the door to him, as she went back running and saying to them, “Shimeon, behold, is standing at the door of the courtyard!”
12.15: They were saying to her, “You are crazy.”, and she was protesting, “It is true.” They said to her, “Perhaps it is his messenger.”
12.16: And Shimeon was knocking at the gate and they went out and saw him and they marveled among themselves.
12.17: And he was gesturing to them with his hands so as to silence them, and he entered and related to them how THE LORD JEHOVAH had brought him out from the prison. And he said to them, “Tell these things to Yaqob and to the brethren.” And he went out to another place.
12.18: When it was morning, there was a great uproar among the Soldiers about Shimeon: “What happened to him?”
12.19: But when Herodus had searched for him and did not find him, he condemned the guards and ordered to execute them, and he went out from Judea and was staying in Qesarea.
12.20: And because he was at enmity with the Tyrians and against the Sidonians, they gathered and came together to him and persuaded Blastus the Chamberlain of The King and asked him that they might have a peace treaty, because the sustenance of their countries was from The Kingdom of Herodus.
12.21: But on a notable day, Herodus was wearing the royal garments and sat down on the judgment seat and was speaking with the multitude.
12.22: All the people were crying out and saying, “These are the sayings of God and not of men.”
12.23: And because he did not give the glory to God, immediately The Angel of THE LORD JEHOVAH struck him and he was infested with worms and he died.
12.24: And The Gospel of God was proclaimed and it grew.
12.25: Then BarNaba and Shaul returned from Jerusalem to Antiakia after they had finished their service and they took Yohannan with them, who was surnamed Marqus.

13.1: But there were Prophets in the Church of Antiakia and Teachers: BarNaba and Shimeon who is called Niger, Luqius, who is from the city Qorina, Manael, son of the rearers of Herodus the Tetrarch, and Shaul.
13.2: And as they were fasting and beseeching God, The Spirit of Holiness said to them, “Separate to me Shaul and BarNaba for the work to which I have called them.”
13.3: And after they had fasted and prayed, they placed hands upon them and they sent them.
13.4: When they were sent by The Spirit of Holiness, they went down to those in Seluqia and from there they journeyed by sea unto Quprus.
13.5: And when they entered the city Salamna, they were preaching the word of our Lord in the synagogues of the Jews, and Yohannan was ministering to them.
13.6: And as they traveled the whole island to the city of Paphos, they found a Jewish man, a Sorcerer who was a false Prophet, whose name was Bar Shuma.
13.7: This man adhered to a wise man who was the Proconsul and he was called Sergius Paulus; the Proconsul called Shaul and BarNaba and he was requesting to hear from them the word of God.
13.8: But the Sorcerer Bar Shuma, whose name is translated Alumas, withstood them because he wanted to turn aside the Proconsul from the faith.
13.9: But Shaul, who was called Paulus, was filled with The Spirit of Holiness, and he stared at him.
13.10: And he said, “Oh, full of all treacheries and all evils! Son of The Devil and enemy of all righteousness! You do not cease to twist the straight ways of THE LORD JEHOVAH!
13.11: Now the hand of THE LORD JEHOVAH is upon you and you will be blind, and you will not see the sun for a time.” At that moment a blackness and darkness fell upon him and he was going around and seeking someone to take his hand.
13.12: And when the Proconsul saw what happened, he was amazed and he believed in the teaching of THE LORD JEHOVAH.
13.13: But Paulus and BarNaba journeyed by sea from Paphos, the city, and they came to the city Perga of Pamphylia and Yohannan separated from them and went on to Jerusalem.
13.14: But they went forth from Perga and came to Antiakia, a city of Pisidia, and they entered a synagogue and sat down on the Sabbath day.
13.15: And when The Law and The Prophets were read, the Elders of the synagogue sent to them and said, “Men, brothers, if you have a word of comfort, speak with the people.”
13.16: And Paulus stood and lifted his hand and said, “Men, Sons of Israel and those who are worshipers of God, listen.
13.17: The God of this people chose our fathers and raised up and multiplied them when they were foreigners in the land of Egypt and with a high arm he brought them forth from it.
13.18: And he sustained them in the wilderness forty years.
13.19: And he destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, and he gave them their land for an inheritance.
13.20: For four hundred fifty years he gave them Judges until Samuel The Prophet.
13.21: And then they asked for themselves a King, and God gave them Shaul, son of Qish, a man from the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
13.22: And he removed him and raised up for them David The King and testified of him and said, 'I have found David the son of Jesse, a man according to my heart. He shall do all my pleasure.'
13.23: From the seed of this man God raised up to Israel, according to what was promised, Yeshua The Savior.
13.24: And he sent Yohannan to preach the baptism of repentance before his coming to all the people of Israel.
13.25: And when Yohannan had finished his ministry, he was saying, 'I am not who you think I am. Behold, he comes after me, the strap of whose sandals I am not worthy to loose.'
13.26: Men, brothers and children of the lineage of Abraham, and those who worship God with you: To you He has been sent: The Word of Life.
13.27: And these inhabitants of Jerusalem and their Leaders did not understand him nor the Scriptures of The Prophets which are read on every Sabbath, but they judged him and they fulfilled these things which are written.
13.28: And when they found no cause of death, they asked Pilatus to kill him.
13.29: And when they had fulfilled everything that was written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
13.30: But God raised him from among the dead.
13.31: He appeared for many days to those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, and they are now his witnesses to the people.
13.32: We also, behold, we preach good news to you, that The Promise which was to our fathers,
13.33: Behold, God has fulfilled it to us their children, because he raised Yeshua, as it is written in the second Psalm: 'You are my Son; today I have begotten you.'
13.34: And so God raised him from among the dead that he will not return again to see destruction, as it says: 'I shall give you the favor of faithful David.'
13.35: Again, it says in another place: 'You have not given your Pure One to see corruption.'
13.36: For David served the will of God in his generation and he fell asleep and was added to his fathers and he saw corruption.
13.37: But This One whom God raised did not see corruption.
13.38: Therefore, know brothers, that by This One, the forgiveness of sins is preached to you.
13.39: And all who believe in This One are justified of all things from which you cannot be justified by the law of Moses.
13.40: Beware therefore, lest that which is written in The Prophets come upon you:
13.41: 'Behold, scorners, and marvel and be destroyed, for I will do a work in your days, which you will not believe even if a man reports it to you.' ''
13.42: And as they were going out from their presence, they asked of them that they would speak these words with them on another Sabbath.
13.43: And when the synagogue was dismissed, many Jews followed them, even foreigners who were worshipers of God, and they were speaking and persuading them to be joined to the grace of God.
13.44: And on another Sabbath, the whole city gathered to hear the word of God.
13.45: And when the Jews saw the great crowds, they were filled with anger, and they were opposing the words which Paulus was speaking and they were blaspheming.
13.46: But Paulus and BarNaba said: “It was necessary that the word of God be spoken to you publicly first, but because you drive it away from you and you determine against yourselves that you are unworthy of eternal life, behold, we turn to the Gentiles.”
13.47: “For so our Lord commanded us just as it is written: 'I have set you as a light to the Gentiles to be life unto the ends of The Earth.'”
13.48: And as the Gentiles were hearing, they were rejoicing and glorifying God, and they that were appointed to eternal life believed.
13.49: And the word of THE LORD JEHOVAH was spoken in that whole region.
13.50: But the Jews stirred up the Leaders of the city and those honorable women who were worshiping God with them and they raised persecutions against Paulus and against BarNaba and expelled them from their borders.
13.51: When they went forth, they shook the dust of their feet off against them and they came to the city Iqonion.
13.52: And the disciples were filled with joy and The Spirit of Holiness.

14.1: And they came and entered the synagogue of the Jews, and thus they spoke with them, so many of the Jews and of the Greeks believed.
14.2: But the Jews who were unconvinced stirred up the Gentiles to harm the brethren.
14.3: And they were there for a long time publicly speaking about THE LORD JEHOVAH, and He was testifying concerning the word of his grace by signs and wonders that He was doing by their hands.
14.4: And the whole multitude of the city was divided; some of them were with the Jews and some of them were joined to the Apostles.
14.5: But there was a decree against them by the Gentiles and by the Jews and their Leaders, to abuse them and to stone them with stones.
14.6: And when they knew it, they departed and took refuge in the cities of Lyconia, Lystra and Derby and the villages around them.
14.7: And they were there preaching The Good News.
14.8: And a certain man was sitting in the city of Lystra who was lame in his feet, crippled from his mother's womb, who had never walked.
14.9: This one heard Paulus speaking and when Paulus saw him, and it was known that he had faith to receive salvation,
14.10: He said in a loud voice, “I say to you in the name of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, stand on your feet!” And he jumped, stood and walked.
14.11: When the crowds of people had seen this thing that Paulus had done, they raised their voices in the language of the country, and they were saying, “The gods have become like men and have come down to us.”
14.12: And they were naming BarNaba, The Lord of the gods, and Paulus, Hermes, because he had been introducing the message.
14.13: And The Priest of The Lord of the gods, who was outside the city, brought bulls and garlands to the gates of the courtyard where they were staying and he wanted to sacrifice to them.
14.14: BarNaba and Paulus, when they heard, they tore their robes and leaped and went out to them among the mob and were crying out,
14.15: And they were saying, “Men, what are you doing? We are also men of passions like you, we who preach to you that you should turn from these worthless things to THE LIVING GOD who made The Heavens and The Earth and The Sea and all that is in them,
14.16: He who in the former ages was allowing all nations to go in their own way,
14.17: While he did not leave himself without a testimony, as he was giving them precious things from the Heavens and sending rain down to them, multiplying their fruits in their times and was satisfying their hearts with food and gladness.”
14.18: And as they were saying these things, with difficulty they restrained the people that no one would sacrifice to them.
14.19: But there came Jews from Iqonion and from Antiakia and stirred up the people against them and they stoned Paulus and dragged him outside of the city, because they thought that he was dead.
14.20: And the disciples gathered around him, and he stood up and entered the city, and the next day he went out from there with BarNaba, and they came to the city Derbe.
14.21: While they were preaching to the people of that city, they discipled many and they returned and came to Lystra and to Iqonion and to Antiakia,
14.22: While they were confirming the souls of the disciples and seeking that they would continue in the faith; and they were saying to them that it is necessary to enter The Kingdom of God by much suffering.
14.23: And they appointed them Elders in every church while they were fasting with them and praying, committing them to our Lord in whom they had believed.
14.24: And when they had traveled in the region of Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
14.25: And as they spoke the word of THE LORD JEHOVAH in the city of Perga, they came down to Italia.
14.26: And from there they journeyed by sea and came to Antiakia, because from there they were commending the work which they had finished to the grace of THE LORD JEHOVAH.
14.27: And when the whole church had gathered together, they were relating everything that God had done with them and that he had opened the door of the faith to the Gentiles.
14.28: And they spent much time there with the disciples.

15.1: But men had come down from Judea and were teaching the brethren: “If you are not circumcised in the custom of The Law, you cannot have life.”
15.2: There was great tumult and debate with them for Paulus and BarNaba, and it happened that Paulus and BarNaba and another with them would go up to the Apostles and the Elders who were in Jerusalem because of this dispute.
15.3: And the church accompanied and sent them and they were traveling in all Phoenicia and also among the Samaritans, as they were recounting the conversion of the Gentiles, and they were creating great joy to all the brethren.
15.4: And when they came to Jerusalem, they were received by the church and by the Apostles and by the Elders and they recounted to them everything that God had done with them.
15.5: But some of the school of The Pharisees who had believed stood up, and they were saying, “It is necessary for you to circumcise them and to command them to keep The Law of Moses.”
15.6: But the Apostles and the Elders assembled to look into this matter.
15.7: And as there was a great dispute, Shimeon arose and he said to them, “Men, brothers, you are aware that from the first days God chose the Gentiles to hear the word of The Gospel from my mouth and to believe.”
15.8: “And God, who knows what is in the hearts, testified to them and gave them The Spirit of Holiness as to us.”
15.9: “And he made no distinction between us and them, because he purified their hearts by faith.”
15.10: “And now, why are you tempting God, so as to put a yoke on the necks of the disciples, which not even our fathers nor we were able to bear?”
15.11: But by the grace of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, we believe in order to receive life, as they do”.
15.12: And all the crowds were silent, and they were listening to Paulus and to BarNaba, who related everything whatever God had done by their hands- signs and mighty deeds among the Gentiles.
15.13: And after they had ceased, Yaqob arose and he said, “Men, brothers, hear me.”
15.14: “Shimeon has related to you how God began to choose from the Gentiles a people for his name.”
15.15: “And the words of The Prophets agree with this, just as that which is written:”
15.16: 'After these things I shall return and I shall raise the tabernacle of David, which had fallen. And I shall build that which had fallen from it and I shall raise it up,
15.17: So that the rest of the children of men may seek THE LORD JEHOVAH, and all the nations upon which my name is called, says THE LORD JEHOVAH, who does all of these things.'
15.18: Known from eternity are the works of God.”
15.19: “Therefore I say, let us not trouble those who are being turned to God from the Gentiles.”
15.20: “But let it be sent to them that they separate from the defilement of sacrifices and from fornication and from what is strangled and from blood.”
15.21: “For from the first ages there have been preachers for Moses who read him on every Sabbath in every town in the synagogues.”
15.22: Then the Apostles and the Elders, with the whole church, chose men from among them and they sent Yehuda, who is called Bar-Shaba, and Shila, to Antiakia with Paulus and BarNaba, men who were Leaders among the brethren.”
15.23: And they wrote an epistle by their hands thus: “The Apostles and Elders and the brethren, to those who are in Antiakia and in Syria and in Qiliqia, brethren who are of the Gentiles, peace.”
15.24: “We hear that men among us went out and have alarmed you with words and have subverted your souls, as they were saying, 'Be circumcised and keep The Law', which we have not commanded them.”
15.25: “Therefore we counseled when we all assembled and we chose men and sent them to you with our beloved friends, Paulus and BarNaba,”
15.26: “Men who have surrendered themselves for the name of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.”
15.27: “And we sent Yehuda with them and Shila, for they shall tell you the same things by speech.”
15.28: “For it was the will of The Spirit of Holiness and also of us that we would not put upon you a burden greater than those things which are necessary:”
15.29: “Abstain from what is sacrificed, from blood, from what is strangled, and from fornication, for when you keep yourselves from these things, you will be well. Be faithful in Our Lord.”
15.30: But those who were sent came to Antiakia, and they gathered all the people and gave them the letter.
15.31: And when they read it, they rejoiced and were comforted.
15.32: And with a bountiful message they strengthened the brethren and established them who were followers of Yehuda and Shila because they also were Prophets.
15.33: And after they were there awhile, they released the brethren to the Apostles in peace.
15.34: However, it was Shila's will to remain there.
15.35: But Paulus and BarNaba did remain in Antiakia and they were teaching and preaching the word of God with many others.
15.36: And after some days, Paulus said to BarNaba, “Let us return and visit the brethren who are in every city in which we preached the word of God and see how they are doing.”
15.37: But BarNaba had wanted to take Yohannan, who was surnamed Marqus.
15.38: But Paulus did not want to take him with them, because he had left them when they were in Pamphylia and did not go with them.
15.39: Because of this dispute, they separated one from another. BarNaba took Marqus and traveled by sea and went to Cyprus.
15.40: But Paulus chose Shila, and he went out when he was commended by the brethren to the grace of God.
15.41: And he was traveling in Syria and in Qiliqia, and he was confirming the churches.

16.1: And he arrived at the city Derby and at Lystra, but a disciple was there whose name was Timotheus, son of a certain Jewess believer, and his father was an Aramaean. -
16.2: And all the disciples who were from Lystra and from Iqonion were testifying about him.
16.3: Paul wanted to take this man with him and he took and circumcised him, because all the Jews in that place knew that his father was an Aramaean.
16.4: As they were going among the cities preaching, they were also teaching them to keep those commandments that the Apostles and Elders who were in Jerusalem had written;
16.5: Yet the churches were established in the faith and growing in numbers everyday.
16.6: But they walked in the regions among Phrygia and Galatia, and The Spirit of Holiness forbade them to speak the word of God in Asia.
16.7: When they came to the region Musia, they desired to go from there to Bithunia and The Spirit of Yeshua did not permit them.
16.8: And when they went forth from Musia, they went down to the region Troas.
16.9: And in a vision of the night, a man appeared to Paulus from Macedonia who stood and begged of him as he said, “Come to Macedonia and help me.”
16.10: But when Paulus saw this vision, we desired at once to proceed to Macedonia, because we understood that our Lord was calling us to evangelize them.
16.11: And we traveled from Troas and went straight to Samothracia, and from there, the day after, we came to the city Neapolis,
16.12: And from there to Philippus, which is the capital of Macedonia, and it is a colony, but we were in this city for notable days.
16.13: And we departed on the Sabbath day outside the gate of the city on the riverside, because there was seen there a house of prayer, and when we sat, we were speaking with the women who had gathered there.
16.14: And one woman, a merchant of purple who was a worshiper of God, whose name was Lydia, from the city Thayatira, whose heart our Lord had opened, was listening to what Paulus said.
